mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and signals accommodative monetary policy, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Conversely, a rising FEDFUNDS trend compresses valuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS slope and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or the sign of the correlation flips across two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above a 0.3 percentage point increase from its recent trailing low, it signals an inflecting labor market that historically precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp above its trailing 6-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE snapshot at 3.5% near cycle lows as of 2005-09
failure_condition: In more than 55% of historical UNRATE-trigger episodes, forward 60d SPX returns are positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and a rising federal funds rate jointly indicate that monetary tightening is transmitting into residential investment contraction, a leading indicator of broader demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Neither series alone is sufficient; the combination signals transmission of policy into real activity.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS rises over the same period
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing sector sensitivity
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show forward 20d SPX returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, decelerating industrial production growth (INDPRO) signals capacity-driven margin compression and impending earnings deceleration, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. The effect is regime-specific: in early or mid-cycle expansions the same deceleration is noise rather than signal.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with slowing real GDP growth
failure_condition: During periods independently classified as late-cycle, INDPRO deceleration fails to precede negative 60d SPX returns in more than 50% of episodes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows restricted to late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer spending momentum that feeds directly into near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Correlation between RSAFS monthly growth and forward 5d SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any rolling 2-year window, or sign flips across consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When trailing 3-month annualized payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 2%, it signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth that supports corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: expansion phase with tightening labor market
failure_condition: Triggered episodes show forward 20d SPX returns negative in more than 50% of cases, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI year-over-year inflation exceeds 3%, it raises expectations of future Fed tightening and compresses near-term equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: In more than 55% of triggered high-CPI episodes, forward 5d SPX returns are positive, or the effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) jointly confirm a broad-based real economy slowdown that is not detectable from either series alone, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations reprice downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth turns negative while PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth falls below 1%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce forward 60d SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances.
